posts._note: Hook: This book is for people who want to become X. Stop the scroll. Claim the reader's identity.
posts.linkedin:
This post is not for everyone.

It is for the person who already knows what they need to do.

And still cannot do it.

The one who has researched the decision for three months when three hours would have been enough.

The one who replayed a conversation from Tuesday on Saturday night.

The one who has a Notion board with 200+ cards and zero lines of code.

The one who watched a career-defining opportunity expire while rewriting a four-sentence email.

You are not lazy.
You are not weak.
You are not broken.

You are highly intelligent, deeply capable, and privately exhausted by your own mind.

Your brain learned, at some point, that uncertainty is dangerous and that thinking longer makes outcomes safer.

That belief is a lie.

And it has already cost you more than you have allowed yourself to calculate — in hours, in opportunities, in confidence, in the slow accumulation of a life that keeps being planned and never quite lived.

Overthinkers do not need more motivation.
They do not need to breathe more deeply or trust the process.

They need a system that interrupts the loop at the neurological level, restructures the distorted thinking that fuels it, and installs a daily architecture that makes decisive action the path of least resistance.

That system exists.
It is grounded in behavioral neuroscience and clinical CBT.
It works in 90 seconds or less.
And it is inside this book.

Overthinking Ends Here — out now via Publixion.

If this is you, the link is in the comments.

#Overthinking #DecisiveAction #MentalPerformance #Productivity #Publixion

posts._note: Why Publixion built this. The origin story. What the system solves. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
We built this book because the existing solutions were not honest about what overthinking actually is.

Every other book in this space tells you to breathe more, journal more, be more present, trust yourself.

All reasonable suggestions. None of them are sufficient.

Because overthinking is not a mood.
It is not a lack of mindfulness.
It is not an emotional problem that dissolves in the face of gratitude practice.

Overthinking is a pattern — a well-myelinated neural pathway that the brain learned to use in response to uncertainty, reinforced thousands of times over years and decades, and now so efficient that it activates before conscious awareness can intervene.

You cannot fix a pattern with a mood.
You need to interrupt it at the neurological level.
Restructure the distorted thinking that fuels its content.
And replace it with a behavioral architecture that makes action the brain's default rather than deliberation.

That is precisely what Overthinking Ends Here is built to do.

At Publixion, we do not publish books that make people feel understood. We publish books that make people measurably more capable.

This one is grounded in:
— The Default Mode Network research from Killingsworth and Gilbert (Harvard, 2010)
— Nolen-Hoeksema's Yale findings that rumination reduces cognitive performance by 30%
— Beck's original Cognitive Behavioral Therapy distortion framework
— Baumeister's work on the Zeigarnik Effect and decision closure
— Gollwitzer's implementation intention research on behavioral initiation
— Damasio's somatic marker hypothesis on the body's role in the loop

Every tool in this book has a mechanism. Every mechanism has a research basis. Every research basis has been translated into something you can use in the next 90 seconds.

That is the Publixion standard.
And that is why we built this.

Overthinkers deserve a system, not a suggestion.

Overthinking Ends Here — link in comments.

#Overthinking #BehavioralScience #CBT #MentalPerformance #Publixion #DecisionMaking

posts._note: Bold contrarian statement from the book. Designed to spike engagement and challenge assumptions.
posts.linkedin:
Unpopular opinion:

Researching a decision for three weeks is not diligence.

It is avoidance in a suit.

Here is what the research actually shows:

Beyond a specific information threshold, more data does not improve decision quality.
It increases decision anxiety.
It expands the perceived complexity of the choice.
And it makes the act of commitment feel increasingly premature — because in a world of infinite available information, there is always one more relevant data point that has not been consulted.

The brain uses research as a delay mechanism.
Not because you are intellectually curious.
But because as long as the decision is in the research phase, it cannot fail.

This is the Information Loop.
And it is the most socially rewarded form of avoidance that exists.

Your colleagues call it thoroughness.
Your manager calls it diligence.
You call it not being ready yet.

It is none of those things.
It is the Researcher profile — one of four overthinking archetypes — using the costume of due diligence to avoid the psychological exposure of commitment.

One person in this book spent eleven weeks researching a camera purchase.
Eleven weeks.
His wife solved it in twenty minutes.
The camera was excellent.
The eleven weeks produced nothing but eleven weeks of cognitive load and three abandoned purchase attempts.

More information beyond sufficiency does not make better decisions.
It makes more expensive deliberation.

Know when you know enough.
Then decide.

Drop a comment if you have ever researched something past the point of it being useful. I want to know how long.

#Overthinking #DecisionMaking #Analysis Paralysis #Productivity #MentalPerformance #Publixion

posts._note: A powerful passage or story from the book. Give free value. Build reciprocity.
posts.linkedin:
This is a real story from Chapter 1 of Overthinking Ends Here.

I am sharing it because it is probably the most honest description of what overthinking actually costs that I have read.

Read it slowly.

---

Michael is a senior software architect in Toronto. Precise mind. Gift for systems design. His code is clean. His colleagues trust him with the complex problems.

For fourteen months, he has been working on a product of his own. A project management tool for freelance developers. He has identified the gap in the market. He has outlined the features. He has a domain name, a color palette, and a Notion board with 237 cards.

He has written zero lines of code.

Every time he sits down to begin building, the thinking starts. What if the market has already shifted? What if a better-funded team is already building the same thing? What if he launches and the early users find a critical flaw in his core assumption?

None of these are unreasonable questions. Every one of them deserves a brief, honest answer before work begins. But for Michael, the questions do not get brief answers. They get spirals. Each question leads to three more. Each scenario invites a counter-scenario. The Notion board grows. The code editor stays empty.

Michael tells himself he is being diligent. He tells himself thorough preparation is what separates successful products from failures.

But on his most honest nights, lying in the dark before sleep, he knows.

He is afraid. And the thinking is not preparation. It is protection.

---

Fourteen months.
237 Notion cards.
Zero lines of code.

The thinking was not protecting Michael from failure.
It was producing it, quietly, one deferred day at a time.

This is what overthinking costs when you do not see it clearly enough to name it.

If this story landed, the full book — and the system that got Michael shipping — is linked in the comments.

#Overthinking #AnalysisParalysis #Entrepreneur #SoftwareDevelopment #MentalPerformance #Publixion

posts._note: Why this is different from everything else in the category. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
Every book on overthinking tells you to be more present.

This one tells you why your brain is physically incapable of being present when the Default Mode Network is dominant — and gives you the neurological mechanism to shut it down in 90 seconds.

Here is the honest difference between Overthinking Ends Here and everything else in this category:

OTHER BOOKS SAY: "Breathe deeply and observe your thoughts without judgment."
THIS BOOK GIVES YOU: The STOP Method — a four-step affect labeling and grounding sequence that reduces amygdala activation and shifts the brain from the Default Mode Network to the Task Positive Network in under 90 seconds. Based on Lieberman et al., UCLA, 2007.

OTHER BOOKS SAY: "Stop procrastinating. Just start."
THIS BOOK GIVES YOU: The Decision Tier System — four tiers of decisions with assigned maximum deliberation windows, the 70% information principle, and a Regret Minimization Protocol for the moment the deadline arrives and you are still uncertain.

OTHER BOOKS SAY: "Challenge your negative thoughts."
THIS BOOK GIVES YOU: The Three-Column Thought Audit and the Courtroom Method — clinical CBT tools that identify the specific distortion driving your loop, test it against available evidence, and produce a replacement thought that is accurate rather than optimistic.

OTHER BOOKS SAY: "Trust yourself more."
THIS BOOK GIVES YOU: The Overthinking Profile Assessment — a 20-question diagnostic that identifies your primary pattern across four archetypes and maps every tool in the book to your specific profile so nothing is generic.

OTHER BOOKS SAY: "Build better habits."
THIS BOOK GIVES YOU: The Daily Momentum Stack — a three-window daily architecture with a Morning Anchor, Mid-Day Decision Close, and Evening Audit that eliminates the morning negotiation and makes action the path of least resistance before the loop can engage.

The difference is not tone or intention.
The difference is mechanism versus suggestion.

Suggestions require willpower to maintain.
Mechanisms run on their own once installed.

Overthinking Ends Here installs the mechanisms.

Link in comments.

#Overthinking #SelfHelp #CBT #DecisionMaking #MentalPerformance #Books #Publixion

posts._note: Answer 5 buying hesitations directly and honestly. Q&A format.
posts.linkedin:
Before you decide whether this book is for you, here are the five questions we get asked most often — answered honestly.

Q1: Is this a mindfulness book?

No. There is no instruction to observe your thoughts without judgment or trust the process. Every chapter delivers a specific, deployable tool with a named protocol and a step-by-step implementation guide. The STOP Method runs in 90 seconds. The Decision Tier System closes every open decision in your life in a single session. These are instruments, not suggestions.

Q2: I have tried to stop overthinking before and it never sticks. Why would this be different?

Because this book does not ask you to stop overthinking through willpower. It addresses the problem at three levels simultaneously: the neurological interruption level with the STOP Method, the cognitive content level with Restructuring tools, and the behavioral architecture level with the Momentum Stack and Decisive Identity installation. Most approaches address one level. This system addresses all three, which is why the changes are durable rather than temporary.

Q3: I am a high performer. Is this for people who are struggling?

The majority of our readers are high performers. Overthinking is disproportionately an intelligence problem. The Researcher and Perfectionist profiles are almost exclusively populated by capable, driven people whose cognitive capacity has become the engine of their own paralysis. This book was built with your specific experience as the primary reference point.

Q4: How quickly will I see results?

Most readers report a measurable shift in loop intensity within the first 48 hours of applying the STOP Method — because the affect labeling mechanism it uses has been documented in brain imaging studies to produce near-immediate amygdala downregulation. The Decision Tier System produces results the first time you apply it. The identity-level changes take 21 days of consistent practice. The 30-Day Plan produces all layers within one month.

Q5: How much time does it require?

The book reads in 6 to 8 hours. The 30-Day Implementation Plan requires under 20 minutes of deliberate daily practice. The highest-leverage tools — the Profile Assessment, the Thought Audit, and the Decisive Identity Installation — take between 15 and 45 minutes each.

If any of these answers moved you from hesitation to interest, the link is in the comments.

#Overthinking #SelfHelp #FAQ #DecisionMaking #MentalPerformance #Books #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
Let me be direct with you.

If you have been following these posts and something in them has been landing — the story about the email that expired, the architect with 237 Notion cards, the analyst who has not slept a full night in seven weeks — that recognition is not an accident.

You know this loop. You have been living in it, in some form, for longer than you want to say out loud.

And the cost of staying in it is not abstract.

It is the compounding hours — 1,000 to 1,400 per year, on average, for a chronic overthinker. It is the confidence that erodes quietly every time deliberation replaces action. It is the opportunity that had a window, and the window that closed while the analysis was still running. It is the identity you have been holding, that says you are someone who needs more time than other people, that is actively generating the pattern it describes.

You do not need more time.
You need a different system.

Overthinking Ends Here gives you that system. All of it. In one book. In 30 days of under 20 minutes daily.

The STOP Method. The Decision Tier System. The Thought Audit. The Information Diet. The Somatic Reset Protocols. The Daily Momentum Stack. The Decisive Identity. The Relapse Response Plan.

Every tool has a mechanism. Every mechanism has a research basis. Every research basis has been translated into something you can use in the next 90 seconds.

This is not the book that makes you feel understood.
This is the book that makes you different.

Get it now at the link in the comments.

The loop ends when you decide it does.
